name: Tree
definition: A hierarchical data structure consisting of nodes connected by edges with one root node and zero or more subtrees.
math_relations: Maximum number of nodes in a binary tree with n levels: 2^n - 1
Maximum number of nodes at level i in a binary tree: 2^(i-1)

name: Binary Tree
definition: A tree in which each node has at most two children called the left child and right child.
math_relations: Maximum number of nodes in a binary tree with n levels: 2^n - 1
Maximum number of nodes at level i in a binary tree: 2^(i-1)

name: Binary Search Tree
definition: A binary tree where all values in the left subtree are smaller than the root and all values in the right subtree are larger than the root.

name: AVL Tree
definition: A self-balancing binary search tree where the difference between heights of left and right subtrees cannot be more than one for any node.
math_relations: Balance Factor = height(left subtree) - height(right subtree)
Number of edges in a tree with n nodes: n - 1

name: Heap
definition: A complete binary tree that satisfies the heap-order property.
math_relations: Height of a heap with n nodes = O(log n)

name: Max Heap
definition: A heap where each parent node is greater than or equal to its children.

name: Min Heap
definition: A heap where each parent node is less than or equal to its children.

name: Priority Queue
definition: A data structure that manages a collection of elements with priority assigned to each element.Where elements are removed according to priority.

name: Graph
definition: A collection or set of nodes(vertices) connected by edges.

name: Directed Graph
definition: A graph where edges have a direction, indicating a one-way relationship between vertices.

name: Undirected Graph
definition: A graph where edges have no direction, indicating a two-way relationship between vertices.

name: Weighted Graph
definition: A graph where edges have weights, indicating the cost or distance between vertices.

name: DFS (Depth First Search) Algorithm
definition: An algorithm for traversing or searching tree or graph data structures, starting at the root and exploring as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.
code_examples: void dfsRec(vector<vector<int>> &adj, vector<bool> &visited, int s, vector<int> &res) {

name: BFS (Breadth First Search) Algorithm
definition: An algorithm for traversing or searching tree or graph data structures, starting at the root and exploring all neighbors at the present depth prior to moving to the next level(exploring nodes level-by-level).

name: Dijkstra Algorithm
definition: A greedy shortest-path algorithm for weighted graphs with non-negative weights.

name: Minimum Spanning Tree
definition: A spanning tree connecting all vertices with minimum total edge weight and no cycles.

name: Prim Algorithm
definition: A greedy MST algorithm expanding the tree using the minimum outgoing edge.

name: Kruskal Algorithm
definition: A greedy MST algorithm sorting edges by weight and adding them if they don't form a cycle/ A greedy MST algorithm selecting the smallest non-cycling edge.

name: Topological Sorting
definition: A linear ordering of vertices in a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG).

name: Hash Table
definition: A data structure that maps keys to values using a hash function(uses hash indexing).

name: Hash Function
definition: A function mapping keys to table indices.

name: Collision Resolution
definition: Methods for handling multiple keys mapped to the same index.
math_relations: collision_condition : h(k1) = h(k2), where k1 != k2

name: Sorting Algorithm
definition: Arranging elements into a specific order.

name: Bubble Sort
definition: A sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order.

name: Selection Sort
definition: A sorting algorithm repeatedly selecting the minimum element.

name: Insertion Sort
definition: A sorting algorithm building the sorted array one item at a time by repeatedly taking the next item and inserting it into the correct position.
math_relations: insertion_rule : insert key into sorted portion

name: Merge Sort
